tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Mammals are animals that have hair or fur and feed their young with milk produced by mammary glands.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: Most mammals give birth to live young, have a backbone and maintain a constant body temperature.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: There are more than 6,000 species of mammals ranging from tiny bats to the massive blue whale.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Dogs are domesticated mammals and are one of the most popular companion animals in the world.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: Dogs were domesticated from wolves thousands of years ago and have since been bred into hundreds of breeds.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Dogs are known for loyalty, intelligence and their strong bond with humans.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Extinct animals are species that no longer exist anywhere on Earth.

tab2.title: Causes
tab2.content: Animals may become extinct due to natural disasters, climate change or human activity.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Many famous extinct animals include dinosaurs, mammoths and the dodo bird.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Insects are the largest group of animals on Earth with millions of species.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: Insects have three body segments, six legs and many species have wings.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Examples include ants, butterflies, bees and beetles.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Marine animals are animals that live in oceans and seas around the world.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: These animals have adapted to survive in saltwater environments.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Marine animals include fish, whales, sharks, octopuses and many more species.
